---
title: "Global Health and Environment GLOA 101"
author: "Byunghwan Son <br> George Mason University"
date: " "
#########################################
linkcolor: red
citecolor: blue
urlcolor: blueß
output: 
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    seal: false
    nature:
      ratio: '16:9'
      highlightStyle: github
      mathjax: "default"  
  #    self-contained: TRUE
  chakra: css/libs/remark-latest.min.js
css: ["default", 
      "css/footer.css", 
      "css/color.css", 
      # "css/xaringan-themer.css",
      default-fonts]
lib_dir: css/libs
beforeInit: "css/macros.js"
# seal: false  
#bibliography: /Users/bson3/Dropbox/latex/mybib2.bib
#remotes::install_github('yihui/xaringan', upgrade = TRUE)

---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# to use FontAwesome
#htmltools::tagList(rmarkdown::html_dependency_font_awesome())
library(fs)
library(fontawesome)
library(vdemdata)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(WDI)
# library(magick)  
library(ggplot2)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, error=F, echo=F)
options(htmltools.preserve.raw = FALSE)
```

class: inverse, center, middle
background-image: url(https://www.cdc.gov/nceh/radiation/training/delete-rad-toolkit/media/images/ClimateandHealthProgramspotlight2.jpg)
background-size: 110%

# .Huge[.Green[**Global Health & Environment**]]

# .Large[GLOA 101]

---

.full-width-green[

# Overview

]
<br><br>
--

# - Primary issues of global environment/health and their relationship with globalization

# - Global efforts and effectiveness

---


```{r include=F, cache=T}

library(WDI)

var = c('CO2' = 'NY.ADJ.DCO2.GN.ZS',
        'air' = 'EN.ATM.PM25.MC.M3',
        'GDPpc' = 'NY.GDP.PCAP.KD',
        'school' = 'SE.PRM.NENR',
        'life' = 'SP.DYN.LE00.IN',
        'oilRent' = 'NY.GDP.PETR.RT.ZS')

wdi <- WDI(indicator=var,
           start=1970,
           country = 'all',
           end=2020,
           extra=TRUE)   %>%
  filter(region !="Aggregates") %>%
  drop_na(GDPpc) %>%
  mutate(developed = 
           case_when(
             GDPpc > 10000 ~ "Developed",
             GDPpc < 10000 ~ "Under-developed"
           )) 
```


.full-width-blue[
# CO.tiny[2] Emission, $ 20 per ton, % of GNI
]
<br>
--

```{r message=FALSE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results="asis", fig.width=16, fig.height=7, cache=T}

wdi %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=year,
             y=CO2,
             group=developed)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=developed),
             alpha=0.25, size=2) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color=developed), 
              alpha=0.5) +
  labs(x = "YEAR",
       y = "CO2 (% GNI)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.title=element_text(size=25),
        legend.text=element_text(size=25),
        axis.text=element_text(size=20),
        axis.title=element_text(size=20)) +
  ylim(0, 20)
```

---


.full-width-green[
# Air Pollution
]
<br>

--

```{r message=FALSE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results="asis", fig.width=16, fig.height=7, cache=T}

wdi %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=year,
             y=air,
             group=developed)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=developed),
             alpha=0.25, size=3) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color=developed), 
              alpha=0.5) +
  xlim(1990, 2020) +
  labs(x = "YEAR",
       y = "Air Pollution (mg per cubic meter)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.title=element_text(size=25),
        legend.text=element_text(size=25),
        axis.text=element_text(size=20),
        axis.title=element_text(size=20))
```

---

---

background-image: url(http://cdn.statcdn.com/Infographic/images/normal/17239.jpeg)
background-size: 30%
background-position: right
background-color:  #f0f5f5



# .LARGE[.red[`r fa("head-side-mask")`] Urban Air Pollution]

--

# What do they have in .Blue[common]?

--

```{r, cache=T, out.width="625px" }

knitr::include_graphics("https://github.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/blob/master/images/polluted_cities.png?raw=true")

```

---

class: inverse, middle
background-image: url(https://github.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/blob/master/images/background_leftEmpty.jpg?raw=true)
background-size: 100%

# All these problems..., are they driven by <br> .yellow[globalization]? <br><br>What's the relationship?

---


background-image: url(https://www.intelligenteconomist.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/03/Environmental-Kuznets-Curve-1.png)
background-size: 40%
background-position: right

.full-width-blue[
# Environmental Kuznets Curve
]
<br><br> 

--

## The relationship b/w <br> .green[economic globalization] and <br> .red[environmental degradation]

--

### - Inverted U-curve shape 

--

### - Global distributive implications

---


# .orange[**Emission is going down**]

```{r, out.width="550px"}

knitr::include_graphics("https://github.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/blob/master/images/economistCO2.png?raw=true")

```

---

class: inverse, middle
background-image: url(https://media.tenor.com/G4XrtEyRKxMAAAAM/baeapes-nfts.gif)
background-position: right
background-size: contain
background-color: black

# .Large[Wait, <br> but isn't it all <br> because of <br> .pink[off-shoring?]]


---

background-image: url(https://www.economist.com/img/b/400/582/90/media-assets/image/20211113_WOC158.png)
background-size: 35%
background-position: right

# .green[**It ain't all outsourcing**]

```{r, out.width="550px"}

knitr::include_graphics("https://github.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/blob/master/images/economistCO2.png?raw=true")

```


---

class: inverse, center, bottom
background-image: url(https://www.mdgsolutions.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/2178_twitter_1200x600_animated.gif)
background-size: 100%

# .Huge[Global Public Health]

---

.full-width-green[
# **Life Expectancy at Birth**. tiny[: Has globalization improved public health conditions?]
]
<br>
--

```{r message=FALSE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results="asis", fig.width=16, fig.height=7, cache=T}

wdi %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=year,
             y=life,
             group=developed)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=developed),
             alpha=0.25, size=3) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color=developed), 
              alpha=0.5) +
  labs(x = "YEAR",
       y = "Life Expectancy at Brith") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.title=element_blank(),
        legend.text=element_text(size=25),
        axis.text=element_text(size=25),
        axis.title=element_text(size=15))
```

---

# Globalization also created vulberabilities.

--


.pull-left[
<img src="https://www.simbasible.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/1-7.gif" width="95%">
]

--

.pull-right[
<img src="https://y.yarn.co/5ad59480-ead9-4d78-8509-c32177040be3_text.gif" width="100%">
]

--
<br>

## - .orange[Global] pandemic more likely due to the increased .red[connectivity].

---


# Global Effort: political salience `r fa("arrow-up", fill='red')` .tiny[(from McCormick 2025)]

```{r, results='asis'}


library(tibble)

df <- tribble(
  ~Dates, ~Steps_taken, ~Focus_goal,
  
"1851–1938",
  "14 International Sanitary Conferences (Paris, Istanbul, Vienna, Venice, etc.)",
  "Standardize quarantine measures for cholera, plague, and yellow fever; led to .red[1892 International Sanitary Convention].",
  
  "1923",
  "Health Organization of the LN.",
  "Epidemic control, quarantine measures, and drug standardization.",
  
  "1948",
  "WHO founded.",
  "International cooperation to improve global health conditions.",
  
  "1969",
  ".blue[International Health Regulations] adopted by WHO.",
  "Legally binding obligations for member states to prevent and respond to cross-border disease spread.",
  
  "1977",
  ".purple[UNGA adopts] Global Strategy for Health for All.",
  ".red[Universal] access to a basic level of health by 2000.",

  "2002",
  "Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria founded.",
  "Attract and leverage .green[investments]."
)

library(kableExtra)

make_table <- function(n) {
  cat(as.character(
    kable(df[1:n, ], format = "html") |>
      kable_styling(full_width = TRUE) |>
      column_spec(1, width = "12%") |>
      column_spec(2, width = "38%") |>
      column_spec(3, width = "50%")
  ))
}

```

---

# Global Effort: political salience `r fa("arrow-up", fill='red')` .tiny[(from McCormick 2025)]

```{r, results='asis'}
make_table(1)
```

---

# Global Effort: political salience `r fa("arrow-up", fill='red')` .tiny[(from McCormick 2025)]


```{r, results='asis'}
make_table(2)
```

---

# Global Effort: political salience `r fa("arrow-up", fill='red')` .tiny[(from McCormick 2025)]


```{r, results='asis'}
make_table(3)
```

---

# Global Effort: political salience `r fa("arrow-up", fill='red')` .tiny[(from McCormick 2025)]

```{r, results='asis'}
make_table(4)
```

---

# Global Effort: political salience `r fa("arrow-up", fill='red')`


```{r, results='asis'}
make_table(5)
```

---

# Global Effort: political salience `r fa("arrow-up", fill='red')` .tiny[(from McCormick 2025)]


```{r, results='asis'}
make_table(6)
```

---



class: inverse, right, middle
background-image: url(https://cdn.theatlantic.com/thumbor/vuGrQmW3MKren03KqCdUxS9v5FU=/0x235:4512x2773/1600x900/media/img/mt/2017/10/AP_16301118100683/original.jpg)
background-size: 100%
background-position: left
background-color: black

# .Large[What are some <br> (fundamental) .red[threats] <br> to public health?]

---

# Democratic Backsliding 

.center[
![](https://github.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/blob/master/images/Son2022_1.png?raw=true)
]

---

# Democratic Backsliding as a .red[Threat] to .green[Public Health]

--

.center[
![](https://github.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/blob/master/images/Son2022_2.png?raw=true)
]

---

.full-width-blue[
# What we know now:
]
<br>
--

# - The relationship between globalization and global health/environment is .purple[complicated].

--

# - There have been global .orange[efforts] to improve health/environment (though the effect is sometimes questioned).
